rationale: The cited paper appears only once, in a methodological footnote, where the authors note prior evidence that hierarchical Bayes and finite mixture estimation can yield similar results in conjoint settings. This serves as a brief justification for their estimation choice, not as a core conceptual or empirical building block of the attentional-contrast argument. The citing paper does not build hypotheses from, test against, or otherwise engage the cited paper's main heterogeneity-model comparison findings.
